items.title: Schools deemed safe after WA's biggest lithium-ion battery fire

children.content: Schools in Perth's south-east have been deemed safe for students to attend after WA's biggest lithium-ion battery fire sparked a HAZMAT warning in Maddington, which is still in place.

gallery.altText: Flames can be seen billowing from a building in an industrial area.

thumbnail.altText: Flames can be seen billowing from a building in an industrial area.

items.title: Demons cling on for epic win over Lions as Freo makes light work of Eagles

children.content: Melbourne digs deep to complete a thrilling two-point victory after a sensational tussle with Brisbane, while Fremantle claims Western Derby bragging rights with a 56-point humbling of West Coast.

image.altText: An AFL player in red and blue celebrates a goal with teammates

items.title: Al Kuwait ships cattle out of Darwin after weeks anchored near Fremantle

children.content: Anchored off Fremantle for weeks because the war in Iran had stopped the live sheep trade to the Middle East, the world's largest live export vessel has ventured north to Darwin.

image.altText: MV Al Kuwait at the Darwin Port

items.title: Couple says loophole allowing theft from cars shipped interstate

children.content: Elihu and Amanda Del Valle say thousands of dollars worth of their belongings were stolen from the boot of their car when it was shipped from Melbourne to Perth, but police aren't able to investigate.

gallery.altText: A young couple standing in a kitchen
A close up photo of a man with dark hair, wearing a black top

thumbnail.altText: A young couple standing in a kitchen
A close up photo of a man with dark hair, wearing a black top

avatar.alt: A profile shot of ABC News journalist Lily Kristanto posing for a photo sitting as a desk with her hands clasped.

items.title: Kimberley diphtheria rates more than triple in a month after 50-year eradication

children.content: An outbreak of the long-dormant, highly contagious bacterial infection diphtheria in Western Australia's north is raising alarm among government officials and experts.

image.altText: A student receives a measles vaccine injection at the Ecole Polytechnique Federale de Lausanne (EPFL).

items.title: The quiet disappearance of Australia's rainfall observation network

children.content: For more than 60 years, Frank Pritchard has diligently checked the weather, adding to a record that stretches back more than a century. But he's part of a quietly shrinking network of observers around Australia. Without it, scientists say we risk missing out on important information that could help improve weather forecast models and climate projections.

image.altText: An older man with balding white hair holds up a rain gauge.

items.title: Aurora chasers hit the road as WA activity slows

children.content: For Leonie and Dean McFaull, fuel prices aren't stopping them crossing the country to add to their capture of more than 100 auroras.

gallery.altText: Red light from the aurora shimmers off the Molloy Island canal.

thumbnail.altText: Red light from the aurora shimmers off the Molloy Island canal.

items.title: Automation is growing in Australia's mining industry — but what are the costs?

children.content: At Australia's largest gold mine, drivers are more likely to be found in a control room than a cabin, but researchers and unions say jobs and safety remain significant concerns.

items.title: Toddler's death at Perth hospital was 'probably preventable'

children.content: The death of a toddler at one of Perth's major hospitals was "probably preventable" and the failure to perform a blood test on him was "more than a missed opportunity", a coroner finds.

image.altText: A close up shot of a child smiling

items.title: Their two families are fabulously wealthy, but it hasn't brought harmony

children.content: Their fathers were school buddies turned mining pioneers, and in another life they could have been friends — but Gina Rinehart and Angela Bennett came to be opponents in one of the most high-stakes legal battles Australia has ever seen.

image.altText: A background image of a mine site, with pictures of four mining figures from left to right.

items.title: Outcry over 'disturbing' video of off-duty police officer pinning 13yo girl on train

children.content: Video of an off-duty police officer restraining a 13yo girl on a Perth train has sparked accusations of excessive force.

image.altText: A young girl holds her head and winces with her face being blurred.

items.title: Country town buzzing as Lance Franklin returns to footy

children.content: Lance Franklin has pulled on the boots to help the Dowerin Tigers, where he played his formative footy, raise much-needed funds for lights and facilities.

gallery.altText: Lance Franklin runs out with his Dowerin teammates for the game against Gingin.
Lance Franklin holds his arms out and yells in delight as fans start running toward him
A football team of boys in striped jumpers, including AFL star Buddy Franklin, wearing medals from a win in 1997

thumbnail.altText: Lance Franklin runs out with his Dowerin teammates for the game against Gingin.
Lance Franklin holds his arms out and yells in delight as fans start running toward him
A football team of boys in striped jumpers, including AFL star Buddy Franklin, wearing medals from a win in 1997

items.title: Crackdown on unregistered NDIS providers coming in bid to save $6b a year

children.content: NDIS Minister Mark Butler will announce reforms to the disability scheme on Wednesday that will aim to save more than $6 billion a year by 2035 — and the largely unregulated market of NDIS providers is at the top of his reform list.

image.altText: A tent with letters which read N D I S stands tall outside with trees in the background.

items.title: Old is new again: Gen alpha takes to old tech with fresh eyes

children.content: With the constant evolution of the digital world, community history is rapidly disappearing into memories and museums. But a historical society on WA's south coast is delighting today's kids by showing them the technology older Australians grew up with.

image.altText: A young girl looks into the rear of a bellows camera

items.title: State guardianship to be investigated in parliamentary inquiry

children.content: The state agencies which control the lives of some of Western Australia's most vulnerable people are set to be scrutinised in a parliamentary inquiry, with public submissions now open.

image.altText: A woman steps into an old, dimly lit hallway.

items.title: Gout celebrates early to win another title, says magic mark 'coming soon'

children.content: Gout Gout waves goodbye to his opponents en route to another national under-20 title in the 100m, as the wait goes on for him to dip below the 10-second barrier.

image.altText: Gout Gout waves at an opponent in the national under-20 100m final.

items.title: The mystery of an outback 'catch and kill' squad during WWII has been solved

children.content: In the 1940s, a shadowy military unit was assembled to catch and kill Japanese soldiers on Australia's remote northern coast. The identity of the so-called Solomons Six was a mystery — until now.

gallery.altText: Four back and white headshots of men dressed in army uniform.
A black and white photo of a large group of men standing to attention in the bush
A black and white photo of a group of men studying pictures of aeroplanes on the deck of a boat.

thumbnail.altText: Four back and white headshots of men dressed in army uniform.
A black and white photo of a large group of men standing to attention in the bush
A black and white photo of a group of men studying pictures of aeroplanes on the deck of a boat.

items.title: 'Indefensible' not to crack down on liquor sales in WA towns, minister says

children.content: West Australian Racing and Gaming Minister Paul Papalia says Newman and Kununurra are in for tougher measures, including closing bottle shops for two days each week.

image.altText: A crushed can of Jim Beam in the shallows of a river.

items.title: Mental health nurse who raped teen patient, filmed 'upskirting' videos jailed

children.content: A former West Australian mental health nurse is sentenced to 12 years in jail after being convicted for persistent sexual abuse of a teenager and making "upskirting" videos of women and children.

image.altText: Adam McGovern wears a hat, face mask and dark jacket as he walks outside the District Court.

items.title: Australian billionaire Andrew Forrest battles Meta over fake ads

children.content: Australian billionaire mining magnate Andrew Forrest is asking a US federal court in Silicon Valley to hold Meta accountable for scam ads using his likeness without permission.

image.altText: A side-on photo of a man wearing a blue suit, white shirt and yellow tie gesturing with his right hand while speaking on stage.

items.title: Taxi drivers call for assistance to survive during fuel crisis

children.content: Country taxi drivers say the fuel crisis could prove to be the death knell for the industry in regional areas.

image.altText: A man standing in front of white van with arms crossed

items.title: Fremantle Prison break remembered 150 years on as 'ultimate story' of hope and freedom

children.content: The story of how a Fenian captain from Massachusetts defied the odds and rescued six Irish political convicts from a prison on the very edge of the world.

gallery.altText: A painting showing several men on a wharf yelling as a rowboat with other men heads into the distance.
drawing of the Catalpa, a three masted ship out at sea
A composite of six mugshots taken in the 1800s of male Fenians who escapes fremantle prison to america
